records.ety: [Ir. & Gael. bog soft, tender, moist: cf. Ir. bogach bog, moor, marsh, Gael. bogan quagmire.]

defs: A quagmire filled with decayed moss and other vegetable matter; wet spongy ground where a heavy body is apt to sink; a marsh; a morass.
quotes: Appalled with thoughts of bog, or caverned pit, Of treacherous earth, subsiding where they tread.

defs: A little elevated spot or clump of earth, roots, and grass, in a marsh or swamp.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bogged (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bogging.]

defs: To sink, as into a bog; to submerge in a bog; to cause to sink and stick, as in mud and mire.
quotes: At another time, he was bogged up to the middle in the slough of Lochend.
